subject: Sociology
title: Ethics in Social Research
description: A micro-lesson that makes Ethics in Social Research usable.

hook.text: Milgram told subjects to shock strangers. Zimbardo locked students in a fake prison. Both studies harmed participants.

buildup.text: Early sociology had few ethical guardrails. Deception, stress, and lasting psychological harm were common.

discovery.text: Today, Institutional Review Boards must approve research involving humans. Informed consent is now mandatory.

twist.text: Strict ethics make some studies impossible to replicate. Yet knowledge from harmful studies still gets cited.

climax.text: The tension is real: protecting participants limits what we can learn, but knowledge gained through harm has a cost.

punchline.text: Science that hurts people isn't just bad ethics. It's bad science.

quiz.question: What is the role of Institutional Review Boards?
options: To approve research involving human participants
